zh.Of, Dalby, Lincoln, England: 英國林可爾史爾達爾比
zh.Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔
zh.lifeSummary: Philippa le Strange - Heiress of Dalby是John le Stange of Dalby的女兒，於1238年出生在英國史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔。她於1250年與Fulk de Orreby在英國英格蘭爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1260年在英國爾哈黑頓享年22歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Philippa le Strange - Heiress of Dalby是John le Stange of Dalby的女儿，于1238年出生在英国史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔。她于1250年与Fulk de Orreby在英国英格兰爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1260年在英国爾哈黑頓享年22岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Strange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): nickname for a newcomer to a district, from Middle English strange ‘foreign’ (Old French estrange, Latin extraneus, from extra ‘outside’).

Irish (Antrim): this name is also common in Ireland, where, in addition to its English etymology, it may represent an Anglicized form of any of several Irish names containing the element gall ‘stranger’, e.g. Doyle , Gallagher .

variants.name: Stranger
meanings: English: nickname for a newcomer to an area, from Middle English stra(u)nger(e) ‘stranger, foreigner’ (Old French estrangier). Compare Strange .

variants.name: Doyle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sean, Kieran, Seamus, Aileen, Dermot, Liam, Niall, Brennan, Brigid, Conal, Conan, Donal.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhghaill ‘descendant of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was used as a byname for Scandinavians, in particular to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDougall , McDowell .

variants.name: Lestrange
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Strange 1 with fused Old French definite article le.

Irish: in Ireland, adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche ‘son of Cúchoigcriche’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the borderland’. Lestrange was adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche probably due to the presence of a prominent family of Lestrange, of similar meaning, who settled in the counties of Westmeath and Offaly in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Dalby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Britt, Erik, Knud, Nels, Obert.

Norwegian, Danish, and northern English: habitational name from any of various places called Dalby, named with Old Norse dalr ‘valley’ + bȳ ‘farm, settlement’. The English surname is common in Yorkshire, where it derives mainly from Dalby in North Yorkshire, but similarly named places in Leicestershire and Lincolnshire are also possible sources. In Norway, it is common as a farm name. See also Dahlby .

variants.name: Dolbow
meanings: Probably an Americanized form of the common Swedish topographicname Dalbo ‘valley farm’. Compare Dalby .

variants.name: Dalbey
meanings: Variant of   Dalby  .

variants.name: Dahlby
meanings: Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from a Norwegian farm name or Danish placename, both derived from Old Norse dalr ‘valley’ + býr ‘farmstead’.

variants.name: Dolby
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and Northamptonshire): variant of   Dalby  .

nameMeanings.name: Philippa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of Philip . In England during the Middle Ages the vernacular name Philip was borne by women as well as men, but female bearers were distinguished in Latin records by this form. It was not, however, used as a regular given name until the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Philippa le Strange - Heiress of Dalby was born in 1238, in Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England as the daughter of John le Stange of Dalby. She married Fulk de Orreby in 1250, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1260, in Hatherton, Cheshire, England, at the age of 22.

fr.lifeSummary: Philippa le Strange - Heiress of Dalby est née en 1238, à Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Angleterre, fille de John le Stange of Dalby. Elle a épousé Fulk de Orreby en 1250, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1260, à Hatherton, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 22 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Philippa le Strange - Heiress of Dalby wurde 1238 in Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, als Tochter von John le Stange of Dalby geboren. Sie hat 1250 in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Fulk de Orreby geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1260 im Alter von 22 Jahren in Hatherton, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Philippa le Strange - Heiress of Dalby è nata il nel 1238 nel Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England come figlia di John le Stange of Dalby. Lei sposato Fulk de Orreby nel 1250, nel Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1260, nel Hatherton, Cheshire, England, all'età di 22 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Philippa le Strange - Heiress of Dalbyは1238年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアノクイン イン オスウエストリーでJohn le Stange of Dalbyの娘として生まれた。1250年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアでFulk de Orrebyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1260年にイギリスチェシュアイアハスエルトンで22歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Philippa le Strange - Heiress of Dalby(은)는 1238년 영국 슈럽실르 노킨 인 오스w스트리에서 John le Stange of Dalby의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1250년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 Fulk de Orreby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1260년 영국 체실르 하더턴에서 22세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Philippa le Strange - Heiress of Dalby nasceu em 1238, em Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra filha de John le Stange of Dalby. Ela casou-se com Fulk de Orreby em 1250, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1260, em Hatherton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 22 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Philippa le Strange - Heiress of Dalby родилась в 1238 г. в местности ноккин ин освестри, шропшир, Англия. Её отца звали John le Stange of Dalby. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Fulk de Orreby в 1250 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1260 г. в местности хатиртон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 22 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Philippa le Strange - Heiress of Dalby nació en 1238, en Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra como hija de John le Stange of Dalby. Se casó con Fulk de Orreby en 1250, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1260, en Hatherton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 22 años.
